title: Risk Register: CDM Adoption Program
summary: The CDM adoption program faces 10 identified risks, with regulatory non-compliance and competitive displacement presenting the highest combined risk scores. Immediate action on pilot authorization and training investment is required.

risks.title: Regulatory Non-Compliance Penalties
risks.description: CFTC enforcement actions targeting CDM-related reporting gaps could result in $5-15M penalties
mitigations: Accelerate CDM pilot to demonstrate compliance trajectory
Engage CFTC liaison for guidance on reporting gap remediation
Implement interim manual reporting for high-risk instruments
risks.evidence: CFTC enforcement actions up 35% in 2024; CDM-related reporting gaps explicitly targeted in recent guidance

risks.title: Competitive Displacement by Early Adopters
risks.description: JPMorgan's 50% cost reduction creates permanent unit cost disadvantage for non-adopters
mitigations: Launch CDM pilot program by Q3 2025 to close adoption gap
Evaluate SaaS CDM platforms for accelerated deployment
risks.evidence: JPMorgan Q4 2024 deployment achieved 50% reconciliation cost reduction; Goldman Sachs and Citi targeting Q1 2025

risks.title: Implementation Cost Overrun
risks.description: CDM deployment costs could exceed $4-8M budget if legacy system integration proves more complex than estimated
mitigations: Phase deployment across 2 desks to validate cost model before scaling
Evaluate SaaS CDM platforms to reduce infrastructure costs
Establish $1M contingency reserve for integration complexity
risks.evidence: Industry estimates $4-8M for full CDM deployment; complexity varies significantly by legacy architecture

risks.title: CDM Talent Shortage
risks.description: 60% of mid-tier banks report no CDM-trained staff, creating recruitment and retention risk
mitigations: Launch internal CDM training program for existing operations staff
Partner with ISDA for certification training materials
Budget for 2-3 external CDM specialists for initial deployment
risks.evidence: Federal Reserve survey: 60% of mid-tier banks have no CDM-trained staff as of Q3 2024

risks.title: Technology Integration Failure
risks.description: CDM integration with existing trading and risk systems may fail to meet performance requirements
mitigations: Run load testing in parallel with legacy systems
Maintain legacy fallback for 6 months post-deployment
risks.evidence: JPMorgan reported 3-month integration delay due to legacy system compatibility issues

keyInsights: Regulatory and competitive risks are interdependent — CDM adoption addresses both simultaneously
The talent gap is the most actionable risk: training programs have 3-6 month lead time
SaaS CDM platforms significantly reduce both financial and technology integration risks
The window for second-mover advantage closes in Q3-Q4 2025 as more competitors complete deployment
